description:
Port of fz-aiz.1 from the unmerged `fz-aiz/any-key-floor` branch.

## Repro (errors today)

```fz
fn pred(x), do: x > 0
fn filter(_, []), do: []
fn filter(pred, [h | t]) do
  if pred(h) do
    [h | filter(pred, t)]
  else
    filter(pred, t)
  end
end
fn main() do
  print(filter(fn(x) -> x > 0, [-1, 2, -3, 4]))
end
```

Errors with `parse/expected-token: unexpected token Else at expression start`.

## Cause

`parse_bp` (parser.rs:1249) unconditionally appends a trailing
`do … end` block as the last arg of the preceding call. `parse_if`
parses the cond via `parse_expr`, so `pred(h) do … end` parses as
`pred(h, do_block)`, leaving `else` unexpected. Single-line
`if n == 0 do … end` only works because `n == 0` isn't a call.

## Fix

Add `suppress_trailing_do: bool` to `Parser` (parser.rs:192).
Gate the call-postfix sugar at parser.rs:1249–1261 on the flag.
Add `with_no_trailing_do` helper. Enable it at cond-position parsers:
- `parse_if` cond (1536)
- `parse_case` scrutinee (1576)
- `parse_cond` clause heads (1613)
- `parse_with` source (1717)
- `when`-guard expressions: `parse_fn_clause`:737, `parse_case`:1586,
  `parse_with`:1769

Statement-position trailing-do (macro DSL forms like `test("…") do … end`)
must continue to work — only cond contexts suppress.

## Acceptance

- Above repro parses and runs.
- 618 unit + 166 fixture-matrix tests green.
- No goldens shift.
title: fz-rcp.1: parser cond-context — suppress trailing-do in cond position
